_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn how attitudes, beliefs and behaviours can affect intercultural experiences positively or negatively, thinking about empathy, inclusion, discrimination and stereotyping.

_source.w: We learn how attitudes, beliefs and behaviours can affect intercultural experiences positively or negatively, thinking about empathy, inclusion, discrimination and stereotyping.
y: Our attitudes and beliefs directly determine whether intercultural encounters are positive or harmful.
Understanding discrimination and stereotyping helps us recognise and challenge them.
Empathy and inclusion are powerful tools for creating positive intercultural relationships.
eg: We explore how empathy — genuinely trying to understand someone else's experience — improves intercultural relationships.
We investigate how stereotyping prevents us from seeing people as individuals.
We discuss examples of discrimination and why it is harmful to individuals and communities.
We analyse how inclusive behaviours create positive intercultural experiences.
We explore the difference between prejudice (attitudes), discrimination (actions) and stereotypes (generalisations).
We discuss strategies for challenging stereotypes and discrimination when we encounter them.
